spec.name: Bog Island
spec.description: A low rise of firmer ground emerges from the bog, its surface thickly overgrown with sedge and a scraggly hawthorn that has somehow established itself on this unlikely patch of dry land. The view from the island is the bog in all directions — black water, hummocks, the dead alders — and the sense of being in the middle of something that would prefer you weren't here. A pile of gnawed bones near the hawthorn's base suggests this island has served as a larder for something that lives in the surrounding water.
